active_paradigms.idea: Per-key Single-Writer Sequencer + CommitHub for push-validated caching
active_paradigms.description: Introduce a lightweight CommitHub machine that all CacheNodes consult for per-key sequencing and commit notifications. For each key, CommitHub grants a per-key token (mutex) to exactly one outstanding writer; subsequent writers queue. The writer issues the storage write via the proxy, and only after eStorageWriteResp(success=true) does it notify CommitHub of the committed (key, value, gen). CommitHub increments a per-key gen and pushes CommitCommitted(key, value, gen) to all CacheNodes. Reads at a CacheNode are served from its local cache if and only if cacheGen[key] == hubGen[key]; otherwise, the CacheNode either waits for the push or explicitly fetches the committed value from CommitHub (one inter-pod hop) and updates its cache. During an in-flight write, reads can still be served using the last committed value because the latest committed value has not changed yet. This guarantees LSI without touching storage for most reads while ensuring scalable metadata (O(#hot-keys) map of (value, gen, pending-writer?)). It avoids range/ownership assumptions and remains coherent if ownership moves because CommitHub is key-based, not range-based.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score via per-key serialization consistent with storage commit order; maximize round_trip_efficiency by serving reads from local cache or CommitHub without hitting the proxy; maintain high cache_hit_rate through push-based commit notifications that keep caches up-to-date.
active_paradigms.cautions: Ensure CommitHub only marks a value committed on storage success; do not allow two concurrent writers per key. Reads must not serve speculative values; they can serve the last committed value during in-flight writes. Use explicit per-key gen counters to avoid stale-vs-fresh confusion across pods. Announce eMonitorProxyRequestIssued only when a CacheNode actually goes to the proxy. Keep CommitHub state compact: map[int, (value:int, gen:int, hasInflight:bool)].

active_paradigms.idea: Proxy-side read deduplication and per-key write gating with fan-out
active_paradigms.description: Enhance StorageProxy to coalesce concurrent reads per key and fan out a single storage response to all waiting callers. Maintain per-key waitlists: on eStorageRead for key K, if an inflight read exists (forwarded but not yet answered) or a pending read is already queued for K, do not enqueue another storage request; instead, register the caller on the waitlist and respond to all waiters when the single eStorageReadResp arrives. Additionally, gate writes per key so the proxy forwards at most one write per key at a time (subsequent writes for K remain queued behind the inflight one). This preserves correctness while significantly reducing proxy-to-storage round trips under read and write contention. CacheNodes continue to update their local caches on responses; combined with gating, this improves chances that subsequent reads can be served from cache when paired with Idea 1, or at least reduces storage pressure if Idea 1 is not used.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Improve round_trip_efficiency by collapsing N concurrent reads to 1 storage read; reduce storage load under write-heavy contention by per-key gating; maintain safety_score by leaving storage as the single source of truth for values.
active_paradigms.cautions: Do not coalesce writes by dropping earlier writes; preserve client-per-write responses (success/error) and ordering. Ensure request-ID mapping: for each read waitlist, track all (caller,podId) to deliver eStorageReadResp and eMonitorProxyResponseDelivered correctly. Preserve nondeterministic forward/hold behavior while preventing duplicate forwards per key.

active_paradigms.idea: Lease-holder redirection with epoch leases to enable cache-served reads
active_paradigms.description: Add a LeaseManager machine that grants per-key epoch leases to at most one CacheNode (lease-holder). Only the lease-holder may write a key; non-holders forward client writes to the holder (inter-pod), which then performs a write-through via the proxy. On successful commit, the holder updates its cache and broadcasts a LeaseCommit(key, value, epoch) to peers; peers mark their cache entries with that epoch. Reads on any pod are served from cache if their local entry’s epoch equals the manager’s latest epoch; otherwise, the CacheNode requests the latest epoch/value from LeaseManager (inter-pod) and updates the cache. Lease transfer/renewal is a constant-sized epoch bump; when a lease changes holder, the manager bumps the epoch, invalidating stale holders. This keeps metadata compact (map key->(holder, epoch, value)) and does not depend on ranges/ownership. Crucially, most reads are satisfied from cache or LeaseManager without touching the proxy, cutting proxy RTTs while guaranteeing LSI by using the lease-holder’s write-through commit path as the authoritative way values change.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce proxy round trips by redirecting reads/writes through lease-holder and LeaseManager instead of storage; keep cache_hit_rate high by ensuring a single hot cache per key holds latest committed data; maintain safety_score via lease epochs and write-through on the holder only.
active_paradigms.cautions: Ensure only the holder writes; non-holders must never go directly to storage for that key. Do not serve from cache if the local epoch < manager’s epoch. Handle in-flight lease transfers by bumping epoch before granting the new lease, and only committing after the holder’s write completes. Avoid split-brain holders by serializing lease grants per key at LeaseManager.

tried_paradigms.idea: Primary-per-key leases with explicit revoke/ack and peer-to-peer reads from the owner
tried_paradigms.description: Have StorageProxy act as a per-key lease manager: it grants an exclusive ownership lease to exactly one pod for a key, and changes owners only via a revoke-then-grant handshake. On CacheNode write for a non-owned key, the node requests ownership (eReqOwn); the proxy sends eRevokeOwn(key, epoch, newPodId) to the current owner and waits for eRevokedAck after the owner drains inflight writes for that key, then bumps epoch, sets ownerPod=writer, and replies eGrantOwn(key, epoch) to the requester. The owner serves reads from its local cache without any proxy round trip, but only when writeInflight[key] == 0 (so it never serves during the window after storage commit but before the write-ack updates its cache). Non-owners forward reads directly to the current owner (ePeerRead/ePeerReadResp) to avoid proxy hops; the owner must refuse peer reads while it has inflight writes for that key to preserve LSI. All writes remain write-through via the proxy and update the owner’s cache on eStorageWriteResp; the proxy also broadcasts ownership changes to both pods so they maintain a compact ownerEpoch map. This preserves LSI because only the owner can mutate a key, owner never serves while a write is inflight, and ownership transfer requires revoke-ack before any new write is forwarded. Under balanced, write-heavy load, keys gravitate to their writers so most reads hit the local owner with zero proxy round trips.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score by serving only from owner with no inflight writes; maximize round_trip_efficiency by eliminating proxy hops for owner reads and peer reads; increase cache_hit_rate because owners keep hot keys locally.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Gate owner-served reads on writeInflight[key] == 0; never grant a new owner until the old owner has acked revoke and drained writes; ensure per-sender FIFO is assumed only for each channel and do not rely on cross-destination ordering; fall back to proxy reads if peer handle is unknown or owner is busy; announce eMonitorProxy* only for storage-facing ops; keep proxy focused on sequencing/leases and queueing.

tried_paradigms.idea: Proxy-validated conditional reads with per-key epochs (If-Current-Since token)
tried_paradigms.description: Introduce a lightweight validation fast-path: StorageProxy maintains a per-key monotonically increasing epoch/version that increments on each successful storage write. CacheNodes track (value, epoch) for each cached key. On a client read, the CacheNode sends a small validation request to the proxy (eValidate(key, epochSeen)) rather than a full storage read; if the proxy sees no newer epoch for that key than epochSeen and no write is currently inflight for that key, it replies OK and the pod returns its local value as a cache hit; otherwise the proxy either does a storage read and returns the value (with the latest epoch), or replies NEED_FETCH so the CacheNode issues a normal read. On write commit, the proxy returns the committed value and new epoch and can optionally push an async eEpochUpdate(key, epoch, value) to both pods to keep caches hot. This design preserves LSI because local serving is allowed only when the proxy confirms that no newer committed write exists and no write is inflight. It reduces storage traffic substantially and converts many reads to a single proxy RTT in the common no-change case, while still guaranteeing correctness.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize safety_score by deferring to storage when epochs mismatch or writes are inflight; improve round_trip_efficiency by replacing storage trips with 1-RTT validations; raise cache_hit_rate by enabling local returns when epochs are current.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Never return OK if any inflight write exists for the key at the proxy; ensure epoch increments only on successful storage acks; include the latest epoch in all write and read responses to keep pods synchronized; avoid serving from cache on a bare local check (must validate with proxy); ensure request-ID mapping remains intact for concurrent requests.

tried_paradigms.idea: Proxy-side per-key write serialization with read combining and last-commit value cache
tried_paradigms.description: Enhance StorageProxy’s queue to serialize by key and aggressively optimize: (a) coalesce queued writes to the same key by keeping only the last value before forwarding to storage, replying failure or superseded to earlier ones; (b) combine queued reads when a read or write for the same key is inflight, fanning out a single storage response to multiple CacheNodes; and (c) cache the last committed value per key at the proxy and serve reads directly from the proxy when there are no inflight writes for that key. The CacheNodes still announce eMonitorProxyRequestIssued but often receive eStorageReadResp short-circuited by the proxy without hitting storage. Per-key inflight tracking and request IDs are already present; extend them to maintain a fanout list for readers and a replace-on-write buffer for writers. This preserves LSI because the proxy only serves from its last-commit cache when it knows there is no newer commit (i.e., no inflight writes); any ambiguity falls back to storage. It significantly reduces storage round trips and proxy-to-db congestion in write-heavy bursts and read-after-write scenarios.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize round_trip_efficiency by collapsing multiple client reads into one logical proxy operation and eliminating redundant writes; improve cache_hit_rate at the proxy-level; keep safety_score high by only serving from the proxy cache when no inflight writes exist.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Carefully maintain per-key inflight state: do not serve from the proxy’s value cache if a write is inflight; ensure coalescing does not reorder writes across keys and preserves last-writer-wins per key; deliver eMonitorProxyForward/Hold/Enqueue signals correctly for combined operations; reply deterministically to superseded writes to avoid dangling clients.
